title: Production Hardening: Redis Scaling & High Availability
subtasks: Scale the existing GitLab Redis instance (gitlab/gitlab-redis-master) or deploy a dedicated Redis instance for the settlement stream (cto:settlements) with Redis Sentinel enabled across 3 replicas
Configure maxmemory policy (allkeys-lru or noeviction depending on settlement stream retention requirements) with appropriate memory limits for production workload
Configure Redis stream persistence (AOF with everysec fsync) to ensure settlement events survive pod restarts
Set up Redis stream consumer group auto-creation and configure MAXLEN on cto:settlements stream to prevent unbounded growth (retain last 100k entries)
Configure Redis stream dead-letter queue (cto:settlements:dlq) with separate retention policy
Create Kubernetes NetworkPolicy allowing only cto-controller and settlement-sidecar pods to access the Redis settlement stream
Add Redis health check probes (readiness/liveness) and configure resource requests/limits for production workload

title: Production Hardening: TLS Certificates & Ingress Configuration
subtasks: Configure cert-manager (already deployed via gitlab/gitlab-certmanager) with a ClusterIssuer for Let's Encrypt production certificates
Create Certificate resources for the web dashboard domain (demo/web) and any API endpoints exposed by the CTO controller
Configure Ingress resources for the web dashboard (demo/web) with TLS termination, targeting the cto-web service
Configure Ingress for the settlement sidecar health endpoint (:8080/healthz) if external monitoring is required
Set up Cloudflare tunnel integration using the existing cloudflare-operator-system for CDN and DDoS protection on the web dashboard
Configure CORS headers and security headers (HSTS, X-Frame-Options, CSP) on all Ingress resources
Ensure the Solana RPC endpoint URL is configurable via ExternalSecret (using existing external-secrets operator) for devnet/mainnet switching

title: Production Hardening: Network Policies & Pod Security
subtasks: Create CiliumNetworkPolicy (using existing Cilium CNI) restricting settlement-sidecar egress to only: Solana RPC endpoint, Redis service, and DNS
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy for cto-controller allowing egress to: Redis, S3/MinIO (gitlab/gitlab-minio-svc), Qdrant, and Solana RPC (when solana-billing feature enabled)
Create CiliumNetworkPolicy for cto-web (dashboard) allowing ingress only from the Ingress controller and egress only to Solana RPC
Create default-deny CiliumClusterwideNetworkPolicy for the cto namespace with explicit allowlists for required traffic
Configure ExternalSecret resources (via existing external-secrets operator) for the operator Solana keypair, mounting as a Kubernetes secret into the settlement-sidecar pod
Set up PodSecurityStandards (restricted profile) for all cto namespace pods: non-root, read-only filesystem, drop all capabilities
Configure resource requests and limits for settlement-sidecar (CPU: 100m/500m, Memory: 128Mi/512Mi) and cto-web dashboard pods

title: Production Hardening: S3/Object Storage for Receipt Storage
subtasks: Configure a dedicated MinIO bucket (using existing gitlab/gitlab-minio-svc) for settlement receipts with bucket name 'cto-settlement-receipts'
Set up bucket lifecycle policy: retain receipts for 7 years (regulatory compliance), transition to infrequent access after 90 days
Create MinIO access credentials for the cto-controller and store in an ExternalSecret
Configure bucket policy restricting write access to cto-controller service account only, read access to settlement-sidecar and cto-web
Set up receipt upload path convention: {s3_prefix}/{customer_pubkey}/{YYYY-MM}/{task_id}.json
Enable server-side encryption (SSE-S3) on the receipts bucket
Test receipt upload and retrieval from both cto-controller and web dashboard

title: Production Hardening: Observability with Datadog Integration
subtasks: Configure Datadog agent (already deployed in datadog-agent namespace) to collect logs from settlement-sidecar and cto-controller pods with appropriate log parsing pipelines
Create DatadogMonitor CRDs for SLO-critical alerts: settlement-sidecar event processing latency > 10s, Redis stream lag > 100 messages, Solana RPC error rate > 5%
Create DatadogMonitor for settlement-sidecar DLQ depth: alert when cto:settlements:dlq has > 10 unprocessed messages
Create DatadogMonitor for operator SOL balance: alert when the Solana operator wallet balance drops below 0.5 SOL (insufficient for transaction fees)
Configure structured log labels/tags on settlement-sidecar and cto-controller pods for Datadog log faceting: task_id, customer_pubkey, event_type, amount
Set up a Datadog dashboard showing: settlement throughput (events/min), average settlement latency, DLQ depth, Solana RPC success rate, Redis stream consumer lag, operator SOL balance
Configure DatadogMetric CRDs to expose custom metrics from settlement-sidecar health endpoint for autoscaling decisions

title: Production Hardening: Settlement Sidecar Kubernetes Deployment
subtasks: Create Kubernetes Deployment manifest for settlement-sidecar with 2 replicas (active consumer group members for parallel processing)
Configure environment variables from ConfigMap (SOLANA_RPC_URL, REDIS_URL, PROGRAM_ID, STREAM_NAME, CONSUMER_GROUP) and Secrets (OPERATOR_KEYPAIR_PATH mounted from ExternalSecret)
Set up liveness probe on :8080/healthz and readiness probe checking Redis connectivity and Solana RPC reachability
Configure PodDisruptionBudget with minAvailable=1 to ensure at least one sidecar is always processing events during rolling updates
Create a Kubernetes Service for the settlement-sidecar health endpoint (used by Datadog and internal monitoring)
Set up graceful shutdown with terminationGracePeriodSeconds=30 to allow in-flight settlements to complete before pod termination
Create ArgoCD Application manifest for the settlement-sidecar deployment for GitOps-managed rollouts
Configure container image to be built and pushed to the GitLab container registry (gitlab/gitlab-registry) via CI pipeline

title: Production Hardening: Qdrant Vector Store Scaling for OpenMemory
subtasks: Scale existing Qdrant deployment (cto/qdrant) from current replica count to 3 replicas for high availability
Configure Qdrant replication factor to ensure data is replicated across at least 2 nodes
Set up persistent volume claims with appropriate storage class for Qdrant data directories
Configure resource limits for Qdrant pods (Memory: 2Gi-4Gi based on vector index size, CPU: 500m-2000m)
Create a Qdrant collection for settlement receipt embeddings if OpenMemory integration is planned for receipt search
Configure Qdrant backup schedule using snapshot API with storage to MinIO bucket
